alignment_status: NOT_EVALUABLE_BLOCKED (6E missing; 4 markets span different venues/sessions; series undecoded)
base_rate_sanity: NOT_ESTABLISHED (cannot compute discovery base-rate without assembled 2024 series; deferred; no computation performed)

A3.1_data_availability_coverage_2024.detail: TWO of four markets lack 2024 discovery data: (a) 6E (Euro FX) is ENTIRELY ABSENT (0 files, all caches); (b) ES parquet ends 2022-12 -> ES has NO 2023/2024/2025 data in the repo. Only GC and CL have 2024 -- and only inside the OOS-named cache (databento_cache_oos, 2023-01..2025-12), co-located with the sealed 2025 holdout. GC/CL are DBN (undecoded); ES is parquet ending 2022. The fixed 4-market scope (ES/GC/CL/6E) over 2024 cannot be satisfied from repo data. Daily row/session counts NOT established (series not decoded/assembled).

A3.2_point_in_time_roll_correctness.detail: Continuous c.0 series exist per-market but are not decoded/assembled into a unified point-in-time daily series with a documented, reconciled roll convention; roll correctness cannot be verified without the (out-of-scope) assembly step. ES (GLBX continuous) vs GC/CL (DBN c.0) roll handling not reconciled.

A3.3_cross_market_calendar_session_align.detail: Cannot align 4 markets: 6E is missing, and ES (equity-index, CME GLBX) / GC,CL (CME metals,energy) / 6E (FX) trade different sessions/holidays; a shared decision-time index + a pre-committed mismatch rule cannot be verified without all four decoded series.

A3.4_target_computability_base_rate.detail: The Step 02 target rule is well-DEFINED, but computability-in-principle and a non-degenerate discovery (2024) base-rate cannot be confirmed without assembled per-market daily series; and per audit guards NO returns/labels are computed here. Blocked on data assembly + 6E acquisition.

A3.5_feature_decision_time_computability.detail: Allowed feature families (Step 02) are decision-time-valid by design (all windows end <= t; label window strictly after t). Computability ON ACTUAL DATA is blocked pending decode/assembly + 6E.

A3.6_no_feature_target_leakage.detail: Step 02 specifies strict feature/target time-separation (feature windows <= t; label window > t; no label-derived features; point-in-time rolls). No features/targets were computed, so no leakage could occur. The RULES are sound and auditable.

A3.7_discovery_oos_firewall_enforceabili.detail: Methodology firewall (2024 discovery vs 2025 sealed OOS) is sound IN DESIGN, but PHYSICALLY the 2024 (discovery) and 2025 (sealed) GC/CL data are CO-LOCATED in the same directory (databento_cache_oos, 2023-01..2025-12). Enforceability requires PHYSICAL separation of 2024-discovery from 2025-OOS before any data step, so 2024 cannot be read without proximity to 2025.

A3.8_2025_oos_blocked_status.detail: 2025 exists on disk for GC/CL (databento_cache_oos includes 2025-01..2025-12) and is presumed for ES; 6E 2025 is absent. This audit listed only directory month-names (availability) and did NOT inspect any 2025 values. 2025 remains UNUSED/SEALED (no step has touched it). CAVEAT: physical co-mingling with 2024 (see A3.7) must be resolved to keep the block robust.

A3.9_scope_guard_compliance.detail: No model trained, no clustering, no signals, no backtest; no returns/R/PnL/Sharpe; no fetch; no 2025 value inspection (availability listing only); no tradeability/OOS-confirmation/live-readiness/profitability claims; no SPARTA_BRAIN/weekly-RS/FRC/BLOCKED_AT_6_GATES/DR-K/Strategy-Lab concepts imported (raw databento price data is not a SPARTA mechanic/gate concept; firewall vocabulary intact).

recommended_next_authorization.exact_phrase: Authorize Multi-Market Bad-Regime Predictor data acquisition + assembly + firewall-separation step (discovery 2024 + warmup only; acquire 6E; decode/assemble ES/GC/CL to unified point-in-time daily continuous; physically separate 2024-discovery from a SEALED 2025-OOS; no model/cluster/signal/backtest/returns; do not inspect 2025 values).
must_include: ACQUIRE 6E (Euro FX futures) daily continuous-contract data for the discovery window (2024 + prior warmup) [and 2025 fetched but written to a SEALED OOS location, not inspected].
DECODE + ASSEMBLE ES (parquet) and GC/CL (DBN) into unified point-in-time daily continuous series with a documented, reconciled roll convention.
PHYSICALLY SEPARATE 2024-discovery from 2025-OOS (distinct sealed directories) to make the firewall enforceable.
Re-run THIS Step 03 audit against the assembled discovery dataset to re-verify A3.1-A3.5 + re-confirm A3.6-A3.9.
recommended_next_authorization.summary: A bounded, SEPARATE data-acquisition + assembly + firewall-separation step (the branch's current steps forbid fetching, so this is its own authorization).

row_session_counts.note: Daily row/session counts are NOT established: ES is parquet and GC/CL are DBN (.dbn.zst), neither decoded/assembled in this audit; 6E has zero data. Coverage reported at MONTH granularity (availability) only.

target_computability_status: NOT_EVALUABLE_BLOCKED (well-defined in Step 02; not computable-confirmable without assembled series; no labels/returns computed per guards)
verdict_reasons: TWO of four markets lack 2024 discovery data: 6E entirely absent (0 files), AND ES parquet ends 2022-12 (no ES 2023/2024/2025 in repo) -> 4-market scope not satisfiable from repo data (A3.1 FAIL).

Only GC/CL have 2024 -- as UNDECODED DBN, and only inside the OOS-named cache (databento_cache_oos); not assembled into a unified point-in-time daily series; roll + cross-market calendar alignment unverifiable (A3.2/A3.3 blocked).
2024 discovery GC/CL co-located with sealed 2025 OOS in databento_cache_oos -> firewall needs physical separation (A3.7 concern).
Target computability/base-rate and feature computability blocked on the above (A3.4 blocked; A3.5 data-blocked).
